author: y4rr
content:
Phase 1C complete. Added ferrotorch-core::signal::windows module (~250 LOC) wrapping ferray-window 0.3.

Functions:
- bartlett(m) — triangular window, zero endpoints
- blackman(m) — Blackman window
- hamming(m) — Hamming (alpha-beta cosine)
- hann(m) — Hann/Hanning window
- hanning(m) — alias of hann() for numpy compatibility
- kaiser(m, beta) — Kaiser-Bessel parameterized window

All return Tensor<f64> on Device::Cpu (windows are tiny coefficient vectors; uploading the result to GPU would cost more than computing it).

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline before claiming done):
- No GPU dispatch added. Module docstring documents this design choice and shows the explicit user pattern: . A fake GPU path would replace ~1us of CPU compute with cudaMemcpy, strictly worse.
- output_lives_on_cpu test asserts every window function returns Device::Cpu — guards against a future regression that quietly adds device-aware paths.
- Forbidden patterns grep clean: no .to(Device::Cpu) inside compute, no silent fallback.

Tests: 15 new in signal::windows::tests covering length/shape, endpoints (bartlett zero, hann zero, hamming = 0.08), symmetry (bartlett, blackman), Hann peak = 1 at centre, Kaiser beta=0 -> rectangular, Kaiser peak at centre, hanning is bit-exact alias of hann, every fn returns Device::Cpu.

Closes the torch.signal.windows top-level pytorch gap (was a 'no current ferrotorch home' from docs/audit/README.md).

The 9 less-common windows (cosine, exponential, gaussian, general_cosine, general_hamming, nuttall, parzen, taylor, tukey) are tracked as future work — not in ferray-window today; would need pure-Rust implementations.

Workspace tests: 4514 passed, 0 failed, 100 ignored (was 4499 + 15 new signal tests). Closes #566.
